meaning: adj.涓嶅畬鍏ㄧ殑,涓嶅畬鍠勭殑
examples: Spoken language contains many incomplete sentences.
This is, of necessity , a brief and incomplete account.
This is a very incomplete part of the picture.
Hugh Henry, convener of Holyrood's Audit Committee, said he had faced incomplete answers in questioning senior civil servants.
Muller acknowledged that many of the stations produced incomplete temperature records and had poor quality control.
